title: Lantana parvifolia
taxonomy: Lantana parvifolia is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Verbenaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the verbena family. The Verbenaceae family is part of the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is further classified under the class Magnoliopsida, which includes many flowering plants that have two cotyledons in their seedling stage. Lantana parvifolia's classification helps to understand its relationships with other plants in the plant kingdom.
uses: Lantana parvifolia has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat fever and other health conditions. The plant's leaves and flowers are often used in herbal medicine to create teas and infusions that help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. In some parts of the world, Lantana parvifolia is used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and indigestion, due to its purported anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, which could lead to new treatments for various diseases. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and uses of Lantana parvifolia.
benefits: Lantana parvifolia is known for its numerous health benefits, primarily due to its rich content of antioxidants and flavonoids. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Lantana parvifolia has also been traditionally used to treat various digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties make it effective against certain bacterial and fungal infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits.
properties: Lantana parvifolia, also known as small-leafed shrub lantana, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and flowers contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. Furthermore, Lantana parvifolia is said to have potential in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the therapeutic efficacy of the plant and its potential side effects.
constituents: Lantana parvifolia is known to contain various active constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant's leaves and stems have been found to contain compounds such as rutin, quercetin, and kaempferol. These flavonoids are believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Additionally, Lantana parvifolia has been reported to contain other bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and saponins. Further research is needed to fully understand the composition and potential uses of these active constituents.

preparations: Lantana parvifolia is sometimes used in herbal preparations for its potential medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of the plant are typically used in traditional medicine. However, there is limited scientific research on the specific uses and effects of Lantana parvifolia in herbal preparations. side_effects: Lantana parvifolia, also known as smallflower lantana, is native to parts of the Americas. However, there is limited information available on the specific side effects of this plant. intro: Lantana parvifolia, also known as Small Buttonweed, is a perennial herb native to tropical and subtropical regions, belonging to the Lantana genus of flowering plants. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lantana parvifolia include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Lantana parvifolia include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
